name: [DOI] Rainbow Stacking Blocks
description: [DOI] Create a beautiful set of rainbow-colored wooden blocks perfect for stacking, sorting, and creative play. These timeless toys spark imagination and help develop fine motor skills.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare the Wood
steps.description: [DOI] Start by selecting your wooden pieces. If using lumber scraps, cut them into various shapes - rectangles, squares, triangles, and arches. Sand all surfaces smooth, starting with medium grit sandpaper and finishing with fine grit. Pay special attention to edges and corners to ensure they are rounded and safe for little hands.
steps.tip: [DOI] Pre-cut wooden blocks from craft stores work great and save time!

steps.title: [DOI] Plan Your Rainbow
steps.description: [DOI] Lay out your blocks and decide which colors will go on each piece. A classic rainbow progression (red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple) works beautifully. Consider painting blocks of similar sizes in the same color for a cohesive set.
steps.tip: [DOI] Take a photo of your layout plan for reference while painting.

steps.title: [DOI] Apply Base Coats
steps.description: [DOI] Paint each block with its designated color. Apply thin, even coats and let dry completely between applications. Most blocks will need 2-3 coats for full coverage.
steps.tip: [DOI] Use a cardboard box to hold blocks upright while drying.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Details (Optional)
steps.description: [DOI] Once base coats are dry, add fun details like dots, stripes, or simple patterns. You can also leave them solid for a classic look.

steps.title: [DOI] Seal and Finish
steps.description: [DOI] Apply a clear, non-toxic sealant to protect the paint and make blocks more durable. Let dry completely (24 hours recommended) before play.
steps.tip: [DOI] Water-based polyurethane is safe once fully cured.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Use only non-toxic, child-safe paints and sealants
[DOI] Ensure all edges are well-sanded and smooth
[DOI] Adult supervision required for cutting and sealing
[DOI] Allow full curing time before giving to children

name: [DOI] Wooden Auto-Rickshaw (Tuk-Tuk)
description: [DOI] Construct your very own wooden Auto-Rickshaw! Learn the physics of axles and wheels as you design and paint the iconic three-wheeled vehicle that navigates busy Indian streets.

steps.title: [DOI] Shape the Car Body
steps.description: [DOI] Draw the car profile on your wood block - a simple curved top works best. Using a saw or pre-cut the shape, then sand all surfaces smooth.

steps.title: [DOI] Drill Axle Holes
steps.description: [DOI] Mark where the axle holes will go on both sides of the car body. Drill straight through, ensuring holes align perfectly.
steps.tip: [DOI] Use a drill press for perfectly aligned holes.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare Wheels and Axles
steps.description: [DOI] Sand the wheels smooth. Cut dowels to length for axles, leaving room for wheels plus a small gap.

steps.title: [DOI] Assemble the Car
steps.description: [DOI] Insert axles through the car body. Attach wheels to each end with a dab of wood glue. Ensure wheels spin freely.
steps.tip: [DOI] Don't use too much glue - wheels need to spin!

steps.title: [DOI] Paint and Finish
steps.description: [DOI] Once glue is dry, paint your car in your favorite colors. Add details like windows, headlights, or racing stripes. Apply sealant and let cure.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Adult supervision required for all cutting and drilling
[DOI] Wear safety glasses when using power tools

name: [DOI] Cuddly Plush Bear
description: [DOI] Sew your own adorable teddy bear with soft fabric and button eyes. A perfect cuddly companion that makes a wonderful gift.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Pattern Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Print or draw a simple bear pattern. Cut out body (2), arms (4), legs (4), ears (4), and head pieces from your fabric.

steps.title: [DOI] Sew Body Parts
steps.description: [DOI] With right sides together, sew arm pairs, leg pairs, and ears. Leave openings for turning. Turn right side out.

steps.title: [DOI] Create the Face
steps.description: [DOI] Attach button eyes securely. Embroider a simple nose and smile using satin stitch.
steps.tip: [DOI] For younger children, embroider eyes instead of buttons for safety.

steps.title: [DOI] Assemble the Bear
steps.description: [DOI] Sew head pieces together, attaching ears. Join body pieces, inserting arms and legs as you go. Leave opening for stuffing.

steps.title: [DOI] Stuff and Close
steps.description: [DOI] Fill bear firmly with stuffing, shaping as you go. Hand-stitch the opening closed with a ladder stitch.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Secure button eyes very tightly or use embroidered eyes for young children
[DOI] Supervise needle use with younger sewers
[DOI] Check for loose threads or weak seams

name: [DOI] Recycled Robot
description: [DOI] Transform recyclable materials into an awesome robot friend! This eco-friendly project teaches creativity and environmental awareness.

steps.title: [DOI] Gather Materials
steps.description: [DOI] Collect various recyclables - boxes, bottles, caps, tubes. Clean everything thoroughly. Lay out materials and envision your robot.

steps.title: [DOI] Build the Body
steps.description: [DOI] Select a medium box for the body and a smaller one for the head. Cover with aluminum foil or paint silver for a robotic look.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Limbs
steps.description: [DOI] Attach toilet paper rolls or paper towel tubes as arms and legs. Secure with glue and reinforce with tape if needed.

steps.title: [DOI] Create the Face
steps.description: [DOI] Use bottle caps for eyes, buttons for a nose, and draw or add a mouth. Get creative with antennae using pipe cleaners!

steps.title: [DOI] Add Details
steps.description: [DOI] Decorate with buttons, dials, screens drawn on paper, or any creative additions. Name your robot!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Adult supervision required for hot glue gun
[DOI] Check for sharp edges on cans or lids

name: [DOI] Balsa Wood Airplane
description: [DOI] Craft a beautiful balsa wood biplane that actually flies! Learn about aerodynamics while building this classic model.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Wing Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Trace wing templates onto balsa sheets. Carefully cut out upper and lower wings using a craft knife. Sand edges smooth.

steps.title: [DOI] Build the Fuselage
steps.description: [DOI] Cut and assemble the body of the plane using balsa sticks. Create a frame that is sturdy but lightweight.

steps.title: [DOI] Attach Wings
steps.description: [DOI] Glue wings to fuselage at slight upward angles for better lift. Use struts between upper and lower wings.
steps.tip: [DOI] Let each glue joint dry before moving to the next.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Tail Assembly
steps.description: [DOI] Create and attach horizontal stabilizer and vertical tail fin. These are crucial for flight stability.

steps.title: [DOI] Finish and Test Fly
steps.description: [DOI] Sand entire plane smooth. Paint if desired. Add a propeller with rubber band power. Test fly and adjust as needed!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Adult supervision required for craft knife use
[DOI] Balsa wood splinters easily - handle with care
[DOI] Fly in open areas away from people

name: [DOI] Miniature Dollhouse
description: [DOI] Construct a charming miniature dollhouse with multiple rooms. Add tiny furniture and decorations for endless imaginative play.

steps.title: [DOI] Design Floor Plan
steps.description: [DOI] Sketch your dollhouse design. Decide on number of rooms and floors. Calculate measurements for walls and floors.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Structural Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Cut out walls, floors, and roof pieces from cardboard or foam board. Include window and door openings.

steps.title: [DOI] Assemble Structure
steps.description: [DOI] Glue walls together, add floors to create stories. Install the roof. Reinforce corners with extra cardboard strips.

steps.title: [DOI] Decorate Interior
steps.description: [DOI] Apply scrapbook paper as wallpaper. Paint trim and details. Add flooring paper or felt rugs.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Furniture
steps.description: [DOI] Make tiny furniture from small boxes, spools, and scraps. Add beds, tables, chairs, and decorations.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Use sharp blades carefully with cutting mat
[DOI] Small pieces may be choking hazards for young children

name: [DOI] Wooden Animal Puzzle
description: [DOI] Create chunky wooden puzzles featuring adorable animals. Perfect for little hands learning shapes and problem-solving.

steps.title: [DOI] Draw Design
steps.description: [DOI] Draw a simple animal shape on the plywood - elephant, cat, or dog work great. Keep shapes chunky with rounded edges.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Outer Shape
steps.description: [DOI] Use a scroll saw to cut out the animal shape from the plywood. Sand all edges smooth.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Puzzle Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Draw puzzle piece divisions on the animal. Cut along these lines to create 4-8 interlocking pieces.

steps.title: [DOI] Paint Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Paint each puzzle piece in bright colors. You can paint both sides or just the top.

steps.title: [DOI] Seal and Assemble
steps.description: [DOI] Apply clear sealant to protect paint. Once dry, assemble your puzzle and test the fit!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Adult use only for power saws

name: [DOI] Classic Train Set
description: [DOI] Build a complete wooden train set with engine, cars, and track pieces. A challenging project with rewarding results.

steps.title: [DOI] Design Train Cars
steps.description: [DOI] Sketch designs for engine, coal car, cargo cars, and caboose. Plan wheel placement and connection points.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut and Shape
steps.description: [DOI] Cut wood blocks to size for each car. Shape the engine with a rounded front. Sand everything smooth.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Wheels
steps.description: [DOI] Drill holes for axles. Insert dowels and attach wheels. Ensure wheels spin freely.

steps.title: [DOI] Paint and Detail
steps.description: [DOI] Paint each car in classic train colors. Add windows, cargo details, and decorations.

steps.title: [DOI] Connect Cars
steps.description: [DOI] Attach hooks to front and eyes to back of each car. Test connections. Create track pieces if desired.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Adult supervision for all cutting and drilling

name: [DOI] Marble Run Adventure
description: [DOI] Engineer an exciting marble run with twists, turns, and drops. Watch marbles race through your custom creation!

steps.title: [DOI] Plan Your Run
steps.description: [DOI] Sketch the path marbles will take. Plan starting height, turns, tunnels, and landing. Remember: gravity is your friend!

steps.title: [DOI] Create Track Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Cut tubes in half lengthwise for open tracks. Cut various lengths. Create funnels and catch points.

steps.title: [DOI] Build Support Structure
steps.description: [DOI] Create cardboard supports to hold tracks at different heights. Build a sturdy base for everything.

steps.title: [DOI] Assemble the Run
steps.description: [DOI] Attach tracks to supports, ensuring smooth transitions. Test with marbles as you build. Adjust angles as needed.

steps.title: [DOI] Test and Improve
steps.description: [DOI] Run marbles through multiple times. Fix any stuck points. Add fun features like jumps or spinners!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Marbles are choking hazards - supervise young children

name: [DOI] Tangram Puzzle Set
description: [DOI] Create the ancient Chinese puzzle of seven shapes that form endless patterns. A timeless brain teaser!

steps.title: [DOI] Draw the Grid
steps.description: [DOI] Draw a 4x4 grid on your square. This helps position the seven tangram pieces correctly.

steps.title: [DOI] Mark Tangram Shapes
steps.description: [DOI] Following a tangram diagram, mark the seven shapes: 2 large triangles, 1 medium triangle, 2 small triangles, 1 square, 1 parallelogram.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Carefully cut along your marked lines to create the seven pieces. Sand or smooth edges.

steps.title: [DOI] Color Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Paint each piece a different color for visual appeal and easier identification.

steps.title: [DOI] Try Puzzles
steps.description: [DOI] Challenge yourself to make shapes - animals, people, objects. Look up tangram puzzles online for ideas!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Small pieces can be choking hazards

name: [DOI] 3D Animal Puzzle
description: [DOI] Build a 3D puzzle that assembles into an animal sculpture. Pieces slot together without glue!

steps.title: [DOI] Print Templates
steps.description: [DOI] Find or create 3D puzzle templates. Print at full size. Choose an animal like an elephant or dinosaur.

steps.title: [DOI] Transfer to Board
steps.description: [DOI] Trace or glue templates to your cardboard or foam board. Mark all slot positions clearly.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Carefully cut out each piece including the slots. Slots should be as wide as your material thickness.

steps.title: [DOI] Test Fit
steps.description: [DOI] Try assembling the puzzle. Adjust slots if pieces are too tight or loose.

steps.title: [DOI] Decorate and Finish
steps.description: [DOI] Disassemble, paint pieces, let dry, then reassemble your 3D sculpture!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Sharp blade use requires adult supervision
[DOI] Always cut on a cutting mat

name: [DOI] Wooden Chess Set
description: [DOI] Carve and shape your own chess pieces, then build a board. A challenging project that creates a family heirloom!

steps.title: [DOI] Design Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Sketch simplified designs for each piece type: King, Queen, Bishop, Knight, Rook, Pawn. Keep shapes recognizable but achievable.

steps.title: [DOI] Shape Pawns
steps.description: [DOI] Start with 16 pawns - the simplest pieces. Shape, sand smooth. Get consistent sizing.

steps.title: [DOI] Carve Major Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Work on Kings, Queens, Bishops, Knights, and Rooks. Take your time on details.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Board
steps.description: [DOI] Mark 64 squares (8x8 grid) on plywood. Paint or stain alternating squares. Apply finish.

steps.title: [DOI] Finish Pieces
steps.description: [DOI] Paint or stain pieces in two colors. Apply protective finish. Let cure completely.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Carving tools are very sharp - adult only or close supervision
[DOI] Use proper ventilation with stains and finishes

name: [DOI] Ring Toss Game
description: [DOI] Build a classic ring toss game for indoor or outdoor fun. Perfect for parties and family game nights!

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare Base
steps.description: [DOI] Sand wooden base smooth. Mark positions for 5 pegs - one center, four corners.

steps.title: [DOI] Install Pegs
steps.description: [DOI] Drill holes for dowels. Glue dowels into holes. Paint different point values on each peg.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Rings
steps.description: [DOI] Make rings from rope (form circles and tape/glue ends) or use embroidery hoops. Make 5-6 rings.

steps.title: Decorate
steps.description: [DOI] Paint the base and pegs in bright colors. Add numbers for scoring.

steps.title: Play!
steps.description: [DOI] Set up, stand back, and toss rings! Highest score wins. Adjust distance for difficulty.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Ensure pegs are secure before playing

name: [DOI] Memory Matching Game
description: [DOI] Create a personalized memory matching game with your own artwork. Great for brain training!

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Cards
steps.description: [DOI] Cut cardstock into equal-sized squares (2" x 2" works well). Make an even number - 24 or 32 cards.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Pairs
steps.description: [DOI] Draw matching images on pairs of cards. Use shapes, animals, letters, or family photos!

steps.title: [DOI] Add Backs
steps.description: [DOI] Decorate all card backs identically so pairs can't be identified from behind.

steps.title: [DOI] Protect (Optional)
steps.description: [DOI] Cover cards with clear contact paper or laminate for durability.

steps.title: Play!
steps.description: [DOI] Shuffle and lay face down. Take turns flipping two cards. Match pairs to win!

name: Kaleidoscope
description: [DOI] Build a working kaleidoscope with mirrors and colorful beads. See the world in beautiful patterns!

steps.title: [DOI] Create Mirror Triangle
steps.description: [DOI] Cut three mirror strips that fit inside your tube. Tape them together to form a triangle, reflective sides facing in.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare Tube
steps.description: [DOI] Cut a viewing hole in one end cap. The mirror triangle should fit snugly inside the tube.

steps.title: [DOI] Insert Mirrors
steps.description: [DOI] Carefully slide the mirror triangle into the tube. Secure in place.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Object Chamber
steps.description: [DOI] At the other end, sandwich beads and sequins between two clear plastic discs. Attach to tube end.

steps.title: Decorate
steps.description: [DOI] Cover the outside with decorative paper. Add your name and designs!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Handle mirrors carefully - edges can be sharp
[DOI] Don't look at bright lights through kaleidoscope

name: [DOI] Wooden Catapult
description: [DOI] Build a working catapult that launches small objects. Learn about lever physics and stored energy!

steps.title: [DOI] Create the Base
steps.description: [DOI] Stack 5-7 popsicle sticks and rubber band both ends. This creates a sturdy base.

steps.title: [DOI] Build Launch Arm
steps.description: [DOI] Take 2 sticks and rubber band only ONE end. Open the other end like scissors.

steps.title: [DOI] Combine Base and Arm
steps.description: [DOI] Insert the base stack perpendicular into the launch arm opening. Secure with rubber bands.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Launching Cup
steps.description: [DOI] Attach plastic spoon to the end of the launch arm with rubber bands.

steps.title: [DOI] Test and Adjust
steps.description: [DOI] Load, aim, and launch! Adjust rubber band tension and fulcrum position for better distance.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Only launch soft objects like pompoms
[DOI] Never aim at people or animals

name: [DOI] Balancing Eagle
description: [DOI] Create a bird that balances on its beak! Learn about center of gravity with this fun physics toy.

steps.title: [DOI] Draw Eagle Shape
steps.description: [DOI] Draw a simple eagle with spread wings on cardstock. Wings should extend outward and slightly downward.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Out Shape
steps.description: [DOI] Carefully cut out your eagle. Keep the beak pointed.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Weights
steps.description: [DOI] Tape a penny under each wing tip. These create the counterbalance.

steps.title: [DOI] Find Balance Point
steps.description: [DOI] Adjust coin position until the eagle balances on its beak when placed on your finger.

steps.title: Decorate
steps.description: [DOI] Color your eagle with markers. Try balancing on different surfaces!

name: [DOI] Paper Airplane Launcher
description: [DOI] Build a rubber band launcher that sends paper airplanes soaring! Combine engineering and flight.

steps.title: [DOI] Build Launch Track
steps.description: [DOI] Create a cardboard track about 12" long with raised edges to guide the airplane.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Tension System
steps.description: [DOI] Attach rubber bands to a crossbar at the back of the track. Create a pull-back mechanism.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Release Trigger
steps.description: [DOI] Design a simple trigger using popsicle sticks that holds and releases the rubber bands.

steps.title: [DOI] Make Paper Airplanes
steps.description: [DOI] Fold several paper airplanes. Test different designs to see which launches best.

steps.title: [DOI] Launch and Adjust
steps.description: [DOI] Load airplane, pull back, release! Adjust angle and tension for maximum distance.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Be careful of snapping rubber bands

name: [DOI] Diamond Kite
description: [DOI] Build a classic diamond kite that soars! Experience the joy of flight with this timeless project.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Frame
steps.description: [DOI] Cross two sticks - vertical longer than horizontal. Tie firmly at intersection point.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Outline
steps.description: [DOI] Run string around the outside of the frame, creating the kite shape. Secure at each stick end.

steps.title: [DOI] Cover with Skin
steps.description: [DOI] Lay frame on plastic or paper. Cut with extra margin, fold over frame, and tape secure.

steps.title: [DOI] Attach Flying Line
steps.description: [DOI] Tie flying string to the intersection point. Create a bridle for better control if needed.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Tail and Fly
steps.description: [DOI] Attach a ribbon tail for stability. Find wind, run, and launch your kite!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Fly in open areas away from trees

name: Rainstick
description: [DOI] Create a musical instrument that sounds like falling rain. Perfect for relaxation and rhythm activities!

materials.name: [DOI] Cardboard tube
materials.quantity: [DOI] Paper towel or wrapping paper roll

steps.title: [DOI] Seal One End
steps.description: [DOI] Cover one end of the tube tightly with paper and tape.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Internal Spiral
steps.description: [DOI] Crinkle aluminum foil into a long snake. Twist it into a spiral and insert into the tube.
steps.tip: [DOI] The foil creates the rain sound by slowing the rice.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Rice
steps.description: [DOI] Pour rice or beans into the tube. Experiment with amounts for different sounds.

steps.title: [DOI] Seal Other End
steps.description: [DOI] Close the open end securely with paper and tape. Shake to test - adjust if needed.

steps.title: Decorate
steps.description: [DOI] Cover with decorative paper. Add designs inspired by rain, nature, or your own creativity!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Small items inside can be choking hazards if opened

name: [DOI] Sock Puppet
description: [DOI] Transform an old sock into a fun puppet character. Perfect for storytelling and imaginative play!

steps.title: [DOI] Plan Your Character
steps.description: [DOI] Decide what character your puppet will be - person, animal, monster? Gather materials in matching colors.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Eyes
steps.description: [DOI] Glue googly eyes or felt circles onto the toe area of the sock.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Mouth
steps.description: [DOI] Cut an oval of felt and glue inside the sock to create a mouth. Add a felt tongue if desired!

steps.title: [DOI] Add Features
steps.description: [DOI] Glue on yarn hair, felt ears, buttons, or other features to complete your character.

steps.title: [DOI] Put on a Show!
steps.description: [DOI] Once glue is dry, put your hand in and bring your puppet to life!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Use fabric glue rather than hot glue with young children
[DOI] Buttons can be choking hazards - consider felt alternatives for young kids

name: [DOI] Felt Finger Puppets
description: [DOI] Create a set of adorable finger puppets from felt. Make a whole cast of characters for stories!

materials.name: [DOI] Fabric glue or needle and thread

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Base Shapes
steps.description: [DOI] Cut two finger-sized rectangles with rounded tops for each puppet. These form the body.

steps.title: [DOI] Glue or Sew Edges
steps.description: [DOI] Join the two pieces along sides and top, leaving bottom open for your finger.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Faces
steps.description: [DOI] Cut tiny felt pieces for eyes, noses, mouths. Glue in place. Use markers for details.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Characters
steps.description: [DOI] Add ears for animals, hair for people, wings for birds. Get creative!

steps.title: [DOI] Perform Stories
steps.description: [DOI] Put puppets on all fingers and act out stories. Make a mini theater from a box!

name: [DOI] Rubber Band Car
description: [DOI] Build a car powered by rubber band energy! Watch it zoom across the floor.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare the Body
steps.description: [DOI] Clean your bottle or box. Poke holes on each side for axles to pass through.

steps.title: [DOI] Install Axles
steps.description: [DOI] Push skewers through the holes. They should spin freely.

steps.title: [DOI] Attach Wheels
steps.description: [DOI] Poke holes in bottle cap centers. Push onto skewer ends. Secure with tape or glue.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Rubber Band Power
steps.description: [DOI] Hook rubber band around rear axle. Attach other end to front of car body.

steps.title: [DOI] Wind and Race!
steps.description: [DOI] Roll car backward to wind rubber band. Release and watch it go! Race against friends.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Be careful with sharp skewer ends
[DOI] Don't aim at people when releasing

name: [DOI] Wooden Spinning Top
description: [DOI] Craft a classic spinning top that twirls for ages. Decorate with colorful patterns that blend when spinning!

steps.title: [DOI] Create Disc
steps.description: [DOI] Cut a circle from cardboard, about 3-4 inches diameter. Ensure it's perfectly round.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Spindle
steps.description: [DOI] Poke a hole in exact center. Insert dowel or sharpened pencil. Glue in place.
steps.tip: [DOI] Center placement is crucial for good spinning!

steps.title: [DOI] Decorate Top
steps.description: [DOI] Draw colorful patterns - spirals, stripes, dots. Colors will blend when spinning!

steps.title: [DOI] Balance and Adjust
steps.description: [DOI] Spin test your top. If wobbly, check center alignment. Trim edges if uneven.

steps.title: [DOI] Spin Competitions
steps.description: [DOI] Practice spinning technique. Challenge friends - longest spin wins!

name: [DOI] Physics Lab
description: [DOI] Enter the Physics Lab and explore mechanics, optics, and energy interactively.

name: [DOI] Chemistry Lab
description: [DOI] Mix elements and observe reactions in our safe, virtual Chemistry Lab.

name: [DOI] Biology Lab
description: [DOI] Explore the wonders of life, cells, and ecosystems in the Biology Lab.

name: [DOI] Math Lab
description: [DOI] Solve puzzles and visualize mathematical concepts in a fun, interactive way.

name: [DOI] Cosmos Lab
description: [DOI] Travel through space, explore planets, and understand the universe.

name: [DOI] Erupting Volcano
description: [DOI] Create an explosive volcanic eruption using kitchen chemistry! Watch the foamy lava flow and learn about chemical reactions.

steps.title: [DOI] Build Your Volcano
steps.description: [DOI] Place plastic bottle on tray. Shape clay or play-doh around it to create a volcano mountain. Leave the bottle opening exposed at the top.
steps.tip: [DOI] Make it look realistic with ridges and a crater!

steps.title: [DOI] Add the Lava Base
steps.description: [DOI] Pour baking soda into the bottle. Add a few drops of dish soap and red/orange food coloring.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare for Eruption
steps.description: [DOI] Make sure everyone can see! Have your vinegar ready to pour.

steps.title: [DOI] Eruption Time!
steps.description: [DOI] Pour vinegar into the bottle and step back. Watch the foamy lava erupt!

steps.title: [DOI] Understand the Science
steps.description: [DOI] The baking soda (base) reacts with vinegar (acid) to create carbon dioxide gas, which makes the bubbles and foam!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Do this experiment on a tray or outside - it gets messy!
[DOI] Vinegar can irritate eyes - don't get too close

name: [DOI] Rainbow Density Tower
description: [DOI] Stack liquids in a rainbow! Learn about density as you create layers that magically don't mix.

steps.title: [DOI] Color Your Liquids
steps.description: [DOI] Add different food coloring to water, corn syrup, and rubbing alcohol. Honey and oil have natural colors.

steps.title: [DOI] Pour the Heaviest First
steps.description: [DOI] Start with honey at the bottom of your glass. Pour slowly down the side.
steps.tip: [DOI] Patience is key - pour very slowly!

steps.title: [DOI] Add Middle Layers
steps.description: [DOI] Carefully add corn syrup, then dish soap, then water. Pour down the side of the glass.

steps.title: [DOI] Top With Light Liquids
steps.description: [DOI] Add vegetable oil, then carefully add rubbing alcohol on top.

steps.title: Experiment!
steps.description: [DOI] Try dropping small objects - where do they float? Predict based on density!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Don't drink any of these liquids!

name: [DOI] Crystal Growing
description: [DOI] Grow beautiful crystals from sugar, salt, or borax! Watch amazing structures form over several days.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Super-Saturated Solution
steps.description: [DOI] Have an adult pour hot water into jar. Add borax/salt/sugar slowly, stirring until no more dissolves.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare Crystal Seed
steps.description: [DOI] Tie string to pencil. Shape pipe cleaner into a form (star, heart) and tie to string. Or just use string.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Color (Optional)
steps.description: [DOI] Add food coloring to solution for colored crystals.

steps.title: [DOI] Suspend and Wait
steps.description: [DOI] Lower string/pipe cleaner into solution. Rest pencil across jar top. Place somewhere still.

steps.title: [DOI] Watch and Wait
steps.description: [DOI] Check daily! Crystals form as water evaporates. Full growth takes 3-5 days.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Borax is not edible - wash hands
[DOI] Don't move or disturb jar during growth

name: [DOI] Slime Factory
description: [DOI] Mix up oozy, stretchy, satisfying slime! Experiment with colors, glitter, and different consistencies.

steps.title: [DOI] Mix Base
steps.description: [DOI] Pour glue into bowl. Add food coloring and glitter if desired. Stir well.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Activator
steps.description: [DOI] Sprinkle in baking soda and mix. Then add contact solution and stir rapidly.

steps.title: [DOI] Knead the Slime
steps.description: [DOI] Once slime forms, pick it up and knead with hands. It will become less sticky.
steps.tip: [DOI] If too sticky, add more contact solution. If too stiff, add more glue.

steps.title: [DOI] Test Properties
steps.description: [DOI] Stretch it slowly - does it pull? Pull fast - does it snap? Poke it - does it bounce?

steps.title: [DOI] Store Properly
steps.description: [DOI] Store in airtight container. Slime lasts 1-2 weeks with proper storage!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Play on protected surface - slime can stain

name: [DOI] Balloon Rocket
description: [DOI] Launch a balloon across the room on a string! Learn about thrust and Newton's third law.

steps.title: [DOI] Set Up Track
steps.description: [DOI] Thread string through straw. Tie string between two chairs, keeping it taut and level.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare Balloon
steps.description: [DOI] Blow up balloon but don't tie it. Pinch the end to keep air in.

steps.title: [DOI] Attach to Straw
steps.description: [DOI] While someone holds the balloon closed, tape it to the straw on the string.

steps.title: Launch!
steps.description: [DOI] Release the balloon and watch it zoom along the string!

steps.title: Experiment
steps.description: [DOI] Try different balloon sizes, angles, or adding weight. What changes the speed?

safetyNotes: [DOI] Don't release balloons outdoors - harmful to wildlife

name: [DOI] Invisible Ink Messages
description: [DOI] Write secret messages that appear when heated! Perfect for spy games and learning about chemical changes.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare Ink
steps.description: [DOI] Squeeze lemon juice into a small bowl. This is your invisible ink!

steps.title: [DOI] Write Message
steps.description: [DOI] Dip cotton swab in lemon juice and write on white paper. Use enough juice to wet the paper.

steps.title: [DOI] Let Dry
steps.description: [DOI] Allow paper to dry completely. The message becomes invisible!

steps.title: [DOI] Reveal Message
steps.description: [DOI] Hold paper near (not touching!) a light bulb or have adult use warm iron. Message appears brown!

steps.title: [DOI] Understand Why
steps.description: [DOI] Heat oxidizes the lemon juice, turning it brown. The acid weakens paper where you wrote!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Adult supervision required for heat source
[DOI] Don't touch hot lamp or iron
[DOI] Keep paper safe distance from heat

name: [DOI] Magnetic Maze
description: [DOI] Build a maze and guide objects through using magnet power! Learn about magnetic forces.

materials.name: [DOI] Cardboard box lid or shallow box
[DOI] Paper clips or small metal objects

steps.title: [DOI] Design Maze
steps.description: [DOI] Draw a maze path inside the box lid. Mark start and finish points.

steps.title: [DOI] Build Walls
steps.description: [DOI] Cut straws or cardboard strips and tape along maze walls to create barriers.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Obstacles
steps.description: [DOI] Include dead ends, tunnels, or challenges. Make it tricky but solvable!

steps.title: [DOI] Test Magnetic Control
steps.description: [DOI] Place paper clip in maze. Hold magnet under the box and move it. The clip follows!

steps.title: [DOI] Play and Race
steps.description: [DOI] Navigate through the maze using only the magnet. Time yourself or race friends!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Keep strong magnets away from electronics
[DOI] Supervise small magnet use with young children

name: [DOI] Solar Oven Roasted Papad
description: [DOI] Harness the incredible power of the sun to build your own solar oven and perfectly roast crispy papads! Learn about solar thermal energy, reflection, and the greenhouse effect while making a tasty, traditional snack.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Flap
steps.description: [DOI] Cut three sides of a square in box lid, leaving one side as hinge. Fold flap up.

steps.title: [DOI] Line with Foil
steps.description: [DOI] Cover the flap's inner surface with aluminum foil, shiny side out. Smooth flat.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Window
steps.description: [DOI] Tape plastic wrap over the hole created by the flap. This traps heat inside.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare Interior
steps.description: [DOI] Line bottom of box with black paper (absorbs heat). Add more foil on inner sides.

steps.title: [DOI] Cook S'mores!
steps.description: [DOI] Place s'more ingredients inside. Angle toward sun, prop flap to reflect light in. Wait 30-60 min!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Contents get hot - use oven mitts

name: [DOI] Dancing Raisins
description: [DOI] Watch raisins dance up and down in a glass! A simple experiment that demonstrates gas bubbles and density.

steps.title: [DOI] Pour Soda
steps.description: [DOI] Fill a clear glass about 3/4 full with clear carbonated soda.

steps.title: [DOI] Drop Raisins
steps.description: [DOI] Drop 5-8 raisins into the soda. They'll sink to the bottom at first.

steps.title: [DOI] Watch the Dance
steps.description: [DOI] Wait a minute - raisins start to rise, then fall, then rise again!

steps.title: [DOI] Understand Why
steps.description: [DOI] CO2 bubbles stick to wrinkly raisins, making them float. At top, bubbles pop and raisins sink!

steps.title: [DOI] Try Variations
steps.description: [DOI] Test other small objects - what else dances? Try different sodas.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Don't drink the soda after experiment

name: [DOI] Static Electricity Butterfly
description: [DOI] Make a butterfly's wings flutter using static electricity! No batteries needed - just science!

steps.title: [DOI] Create Body
steps.description: [DOI] Cut a butterfly body shape from cardstock. Make it sturdy - this is the base.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Tissue Wings
steps.description: [DOI] Cut delicate wing shapes from tissue paper. Make them light and thin.

steps.title: [DOI] Attach Wings
steps.description: [DOI] Tape wings to body at center only. Wings should be free to move on edges.

steps.title: [DOI] Charge Balloon
steps.description: [DOI] Blow up balloon and rub vigorously on your hair or wool sweater.

steps.title: [DOI] Make Wings Flutter
steps.description: [DOI] Hold charged balloon near tissue wings - watch them rise toward it! Move balloon to make them flutter.

name: [DOI] Water Cycle in a Bag
description: [DOI] Create a mini water cycle in a plastic bag! Watch evaporation, condensation, and precipitation happen.

steps.title: [DOI] Draw Scene
steps.description: [DOI] Draw clouds at top of bag, sun, and waves at bottom using permanent marker.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Water
steps.description: [DOI] Pour water into bag with blue food coloring. This is your ocean!

steps.title: [DOI] Seal and Mount
steps.description: [DOI] Seal bag tightly. Tape to sunny window at eye level.

steps.title: [DOI] Observe Changes
steps.description: [DOI] Check throughout the day. Water evaporates, condenses on bag, and "rains" down!

steps.title: [DOI] Label the Cycle
steps.description: [DOI] Add labels: evaporation (water rising), condensation (droplets forming), precipitation (drops falling).

safetyNotes: [DOI] Ensure bag is sealed to prevent leaks

name: [DOI] Egg Drop Challenge
description: [DOI] Design a contraption to protect an egg from a high drop! Classic engineering challenge that teaches impact absorption.

steps.title: [DOI] Study the Problem
steps.description: [DOI] Understand forces: impact, gravity, momentum. What slows falls? What absorbs shock?

steps.title: [DOI] Design Your Device
steps.description: [DOI] Sketch ideas. Consider: padding, parachute, crumple zones, suspension systems.

steps.title: [DOI] Build Prototype
steps.description: [DOI] Construct your egg protection device. Secure egg inside with cushioning materials.

steps.title: [DOI] Test Drop
steps.description: [DOI] Start from low height. If successful, go higher! Document results.

steps.title: Iterate
steps.description: [DOI] If egg breaks, analyze why. Improve design and test again!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Do drops outside - eggs are messy!
[DOI] Wash hands after handling raw eggs

name: [DOI] Finger Paint Masterpiece
description: [DOI] Get messy with finger paints! Create colorful artwork while developing fine motor skills and sensory exploration.

steps.title: [DOI] Set Up Space
steps.description: [DOI] Cover table with plastic. Put on smock. Tape paper down so it doesn't move.

steps.title: [DOI] Explore Colors
steps.description: [DOI] Start with one color. Dip fingers and make marks. Try dots, lines, swirls!

steps.title: [DOI] Mix Colors
steps.description: [DOI] Add another color. See what happens when they blend! Yellow + blue = ?

steps.title: [DOI] Try Techniques
steps.description: [DOI] Use whole hand, just fingertips, sides of hands. Make prints and patterns.

steps.title: [DOI] Clean Up
steps.description: [DOI] Wash hands. Let painting dry flat. Display your masterpiece!

name: [DOI] Nature Collage
description: [DOI] Collect treasures from nature and arrange them into beautiful artwork! Combines outdoor exploration with creativity.

steps.title: [DOI] Nature Walk
steps.description: [DOI] Go outside and collect natural items: leaves, petals, small twigs, seeds, grass.
steps.tip: [DOI] Look for different colors, textures, and shapes!

steps.title: [DOI] Sort Treasures
steps.description: [DOI] Lay out your collection. Group by color, size, or type.

steps.title: [DOI] Plan Design
steps.description: [DOI] Arrange items on your paper WITHOUT gluing. Try different layouts.

steps.title: [DOI] Glue in Place
steps.description: [DOI] Once happy with design, glue items down one by one. Press gently.

steps.title: [DOI] Preserve (Optional)
steps.description: [DOI] Cover with clear contact paper to preserve, or let dry and display!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Check for insects before bringing items inside

name: [DOI] Bandhani Tie-Dye Dupatta
description: [DOI] Discover the ancient Indian art of Bandhani! Use rubber bands and vibrant natural dyes to create mesmerizing geometric patterns on a cotton dupatta or scarf.

steps.title: [DOI] Wet the Shirt
steps.description: [DOI] Soak shirt in water and wring out until damp. This helps dye spread evenly.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Pattern
steps.description: [DOI] For spiral: pinch center and twist entire shirt. For stripes: fold accordion-style. Secure with rubber bands.
steps.tip: [DOI] Tighter bands = sharper lines. More bands = more white space.

steps.title: [DOI] Mix Dyes
steps.description: [DOI] Prepare dyes according to package. Wear gloves from this point on!

steps.title: [DOI] Apply Color
steps.description: [DOI] Squeeze dye onto shirt sections. Use different colors in different areas. Flip and do back too.

steps.title: [DOI] Wait and Rinse
steps.description: [DOI] Bag the shirt and wait 6-24 hours. Rinse under cold water until water runs clear. Remove bands and wash.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Work outdoors or on protected surface

name: [DOI] Paper Plate Animals
description: [DOI] Transform simple paper plates into adorable animals! Great for parties or imaginative play.

steps.title: [DOI] Choose Animal
steps.description: [DOI] Pick your favorite animal! Lion, pig, frog, cat, and owl are great starters.

steps.title: [DOI] Paint Base
steps.description: [DOI] Paint the plate the main color of your animal. Let dry.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Features
steps.description: [DOI] Cut ears, noses, whiskers, beaks from construction paper.

steps.title: [DOI] Assemble Face
steps.description: [DOI] Glue on googly eyes and paper features. Add details with markers.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Extras
steps.description: [DOI] Attach popsicle stick for a mask, or hang as decoration!

name: [DOI] Rock Painting
description: [DOI] Turn ordinary rocks into colorful treasures! Paint animals, patterns, or inspirational words.

steps.title: [DOI] Find Rocks
steps.description: [DOI] Look for smooth, flat rocks. Wash and dry them completely.

steps.title: [DOI] Plan Design
steps.description: [DOI] Lightly sketch your design with pencil. Animals, mandalas, and words work great!

steps.title: [DOI] Paint Base
steps.description: [DOI] Paint background color. Let dry completely before adding details.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Details
steps.description: [DOI] Paint your design. Start with larger shapes, add small details last.

steps.title: [DOI] Seal and Share
steps.description: [DOI] Optional: apply clear coat. Hide rocks for others to find or keep as decoration!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Use acrylic paints - they're waterproof when dry

name: [DOI] Origami Zoo
description: [DOI] Fold paper into amazing animals with the ancient art of origami! Create a whole zoo of creatures.

steps.title: [DOI] Start Simple
steps.description: [DOI] Begin with an easy fold like a dog face or fish. Learn basic folds: valley, mountain, squash.

steps.title: [DOI] Practice Precision
steps.description: [DOI] Fold carefully - crisp creases make better animals! Line up edges exactly.
steps.tip: [DOI] Use fingernail to crease sharply.

steps.title: [DOI] Try Animals
steps.description: [DOI] Make a frog that jumps! A bird that flaps! A crane for good luck!

steps.title: [DOI] Add Details
steps.description: [DOI] Use markers to add eyes, spots, or patterns to your animals.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Display
steps.description: [DOI] Arrange your zoo on a shelf or make a mobile to hang!

name: [DOI] Salt Painting
description: [DOI] Create magical raised designs using glue and salt, then add color and watch it spread!

steps.title: [DOI] Draw with Glue
steps.description: [DOI] Squeeze glue in lines to create a design - rainbow, name, shapes. Keep lines thick.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Salt
steps.description: [DOI] While glue is wet, pour salt over entire design. Shake off excess.

steps.title: [DOI] Let Set
steps.description: [DOI] Wait a few minutes for glue to start setting with salt stuck on.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Color
steps.description: [DOI] Drop liquid color onto salt. Watch it spread along the lines like magic!

steps.title: [DOI] Dry and Display
steps.description: [DOI] Let dry completely (overnight is best). Handle gently - salt can fall off.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Don't eat the salt after coloring

name: [DOI] Terracotta Clay Jewelry
description: [DOI] Connect with ancient Harappan traditions by sculpting your own terracotta jewelry! Shape natural clay into beautiful beads, dry them in the sun, and paint them with traditional Indian motifs.

steps.title: [DOI] Color Pasta
steps.description: [DOI] Paint pasta or shake in bags with food coloring and rubbing alcohol. Let dry completely.

steps.title: [DOI] Plan Pattern
steps.description: [DOI] Lay out pasta beads and plan your color pattern before stringing.

steps.title: [DOI] String Beads
steps.description: [DOI] Thread pasta onto string. Tie a bead at one end first to stop others falling off.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Closure
steps.description: [DOI] Tie ends together for necklace, or add clasps for fancier finish.

steps.title: [DOI] Model and Gift
steps.description: [DOI] Wear your creation proudly or make gifts for friends and family!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Pasta jewelry is not edible after painting

name: [DOI] Scratch Art Cards
description: [DOI] Create rainbow scratch art from scratch! Layer colors, cover with black, then scratch to reveal hidden rainbows.

materials.name: [DOI] Black acrylic paint or black crayon

steps.title: [DOI] Color Base Layer
steps.description: [DOI] Cover entire paper with heavy crayon in bright colors. Press hard! No white showing.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Black Layer
steps.description: [DOI] Mix tiny bit of dish soap into black paint. Paint over all the crayon. Or use black crayon (harder work!).

steps.title: [DOI] Let Dry
steps.description: [DOI] If using paint, let dry completely before scratching.

steps.title: [DOI] Scratch Design
steps.description: [DOI] Use toothpick to scratch away black, revealing rainbow colors beneath!

steps.title: [DOI] Create Cards
steps.description: [DOI] Make greeting cards, bookmarks, or framed art with your scratch designs!

name: [DOI] Tissue Paper Suncatcher
description: [DOI] Create stained glass-style art that catches sunlight! Beautiful colors glow when hung in a window.

steps.title: [DOI] Cut Frame
steps.description: [DOI] Cut a frame shape from black cardstock - circle, heart, or rectangle with center removed.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare Contact Paper
steps.description: [DOI] Cut contact paper slightly larger than frame. Peel backing and lay sticky side up.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Tissue Paper
steps.description: [DOI] Tear or cut tissue paper pieces. Press onto contact paper in colorful pattern.

steps.title: [DOI] Seal and Frame
steps.description: [DOI] Place second contact paper on top, sticky side down. Press to seal. Trim to frame and attach frame.

steps.title: [DOI] Hang in Window
steps.description: [DOI] Punch hole in top, add string, and hang where sunlight will shine through!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Contact paper is tricky - take your time

name: [DOI] Clay Pinch Pots
description: [DOI] Shape air-dry clay into beautiful bowls and pots using the ancient pinch pot technique!

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare Clay
steps.description: [DOI] Knead clay until soft and pliable. Roll into a ball about the size of your fist.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Opening
steps.description: [DOI] Push thumb into center of ball. Slowly rotate and pinch walls between thumb (inside) and fingers (outside).

steps.title: [DOI] Shape the Pot
steps.description: [DOI] Keep pinching and rotating. Even thickness is key! Smooth cracks with wet finger.
steps.tip: [DOI] Keep walls no thinner than 1/4 inch.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Details
steps.description: [DOI] Create texture with tools. Add handles, feet, or decorative elements.

steps.title: [DOI] Dry and Paint
steps.description: [DOI] Let dry completely (2-3 days). Paint with acrylics once fully dried.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Handle dried pieces gently - they're fragile
[DOI] Not food-safe unless using special glazes

name: [DOI] Collaborative Mural
description: [DOI] Create a large artwork together! Everyone contributes to one big painting.

materials.name: [DOI] Large paper roll or joined sheets

steps.title: [DOI] Set Up Canvas
steps.description: [DOI] Roll out paper on floor or tape to wall. Make it BIG - this is a group project!

steps.title: [DOI] Choose Theme
steps.description: [DOI] Decide on a theme together: underwater, space, garden, cityscape, or abstract!

steps.title: [DOI] Assign Sections
steps.description: [DOI] Each person gets an area to work on. Plan how sections will connect.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Together
steps.description: [DOI] Everyone works simultaneously! Add to each other's areas if invited.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Final Touches
steps.description: [DOI] Step back, see what's missing. Add connecting elements together.

name: [DOI] Nature Scavenger Hunt
description: [DOI] Explore the outdoors with a checklist of natural treasures to find! Perfect for backyards, parks, or nature trails.

steps.title: [DOI] Print Checklist
steps.description: [DOI] Use our checklist or create your own: pinecone, feather, smooth rock, flower, etc.

steps.title: [DOI] Go Outside!
steps.description: [DOI] Head to backyard, park, or nature trail. Bring your checklist and collection bag.

steps.title: [DOI] Start Hunting
steps.description: [DOI] Check off items as you find them. Collect items you can take (no picking protected plants!).
steps.tip: [DOI] Look up, down, and all around - treasures are everywhere!

steps.title: [DOI] Observe Closely
steps.description: [DOI] Use magnifying glass to examine finds. What details do you notice?

steps.title: [DOI] Share Discoveries
steps.description: [DOI] Show what you found! Learn names of plants and animals you discovered.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Stay on paths and in sight of adults
[DOI] Don't touch unknown plants or animals

name: [DOI] Bug Hotel
description: [DOI] Build a cozy habitat for beneficial insects! Create shelter for ladybugs, bees, and other garden helpers.

steps.title: [DOI] Find a Base
steps.description: [DOI] Use a clean tin can, small wooden box, or bundle of sticks tied together.

steps.title: [DOI] Gather Materials
steps.description: [DOI] Collect natural materials: hollow sticks, pinecones, bark, dried leaves, etc.

steps.title: [DOI] Fill the Hotel
steps.description: [DOI] Pack materials tightly into your base. Different insects like different spaces!
steps.tip: [DOI] Hollow tubes for solitary bees, pinecones for ladybugs.

steps.title: [DOI] Secure Contents
steps.description: [DOI] Make sure everything is packed tight. Add wire or more sticks if needed.

steps.title: [DOI] Place in Garden
steps.description: [DOI] Position in a sheltered spot facing south. Off the ground prevents flooding.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Don't disturb guests once they move in
[DOI] Check for existing insect nests before gathering materials

name: [DOI] Pressed Flower Art
description: [DOI] Preserve beautiful flowers and leaves by pressing! Create bookmarks, cards, or framed art.

steps.title: [DOI] Collect Specimens
steps.description: [DOI] Pick flowers and leaves on a dry day. Choose flat flowers or ones you can flatten.

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare for Pressing
steps.description: [DOI] Place flowers between parchment paper. Arrange flat - don't overlap.

steps.title: [DOI] Press in Books
steps.description: [DOI] Put paper with flowers inside heavy book. Stack more books on top.

steps.title: [DOI] Wait Patiently
steps.description: [DOI] Leave for 1-2 weeks. Check occasionally. Change paper if damp.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Art
steps.description: [DOI] Carefully remove pressed flowers. Arrange on cards, bookmarks, or frame!

name: [DOI] Coconut Shell Bird Feeder
description: [DOI] Repurpose an empty coconut shell into a beautiful, hanging bird feeder! Fill it with local grains and watch the incredible variety of native birds flock to your garden.

steps.title: [DOI] Tie Hanger
steps.description: [DOI] Tie string securely around top of pinecone for hanging.

steps.title: [DOI] Apply Peanut Butter
steps.description: [DOI] Spread peanut butter all over pinecone, getting into the scales.
steps.tip: [DOI] Use a butter knife or your hands (messy but fun!).

steps.title: [DOI] Add Seeds
steps.description: [DOI] Roll coated pinecone in birdseed, pressing seeds into peanut butter.

steps.title: [DOI] Hang Outside
steps.description: [DOI] Hang from a tree branch where you can watch from a window.

steps.title: [DOI] Bird Watch
steps.description: [DOI] Keep a journal of birds that visit! Learn their names and songs.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Check for nut allergies before using peanut butter
[DOI] Place where squirrels can't easily reach

name: [DOI] Mud Kitchen Play
description: [DOI] Set up an outdoor play kitchen for messy, imaginative mud cooking! A classic childhood experience.

steps.title: [DOI] Set Up Kitchen
steps.description: [DOI] Find a good spot near dirt and water. Arrange your tools. A small table helps!

steps.title: [DOI] Prepare Ingredients
steps.description: [DOI] Gather water, dirt, and natural add-ins like leaves, petals, seeds.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Recipes
steps.description: [DOI] Make mud pies, dirt soup, nature stew! Mix, stir, pour, and create.

steps.title: [DOI] Serve and Play
steps.description: [DOI] Set up a pretend restaurant. Serve your creations to stuffed animal guests!

steps.title: [DOI] Clean Up
steps.description: [DOI] Hose off tools and hands. Some dirty play is good!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Check area for broken glass or harmful items

name: [DOI] Shadow Tracing Art
description: [DOI] Use the sun to create art! Trace shadows of objects, toys, or even friends for unique silhouette artwork.

steps.title: [DOI] Find Shadows
steps.description: [DOI] Go outside on a sunny day. Notice how everything has a shadow!

steps.title: [DOI] Position Objects
steps.description: [DOI] Place toys, plants, or pose yourself to create interesting shadow shapes.

steps.title: [DOI] Trace Outlines
steps.description: [DOI] Use chalk to trace around shadow edges on pavement.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Details
steps.description: [DOI] Color in your traced shadows. Add features, patterns, or backgrounds.

steps.title: [DOI] Notice Changes
steps.description: [DOI] Come back later - have shadows moved? Try tracing the same object at different times!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Don't look directly at the sun

name: [DOI] Cloud Watching Journal
description: [DOI] Lie back and watch the clouds! Learn cloud types, spot shapes, and create an imagination journal.

steps.title: [DOI] Find a Spot
steps.description: [DOI] Lay blanket in open area with good sky view. Lie on your back.

steps.title: [DOI] Observe Clouds
steps.description: [DOI] Watch the clouds move. What shapes do you see? Animals? Faces? Objects?

steps.title: [DOI] Learn Cloud Types
steps.description: [DOI] Identify clouds: fluffy cumulus, wispy cirrus, layered stratus. What's up there today?

steps.title: [DOI] Draw and Write
steps.description: [DOI] Sketch the shapes you see. Write stories about cloud characters.

steps.title: [DOI] Predict Weather
steps.description: [DOI] Different clouds mean different weather. What do today's clouds tell us?

name: [DOI] Fairy Garden
description: [DOI] Create a miniature magical garden for fairies! Use tiny plants, pebbles, and handmade accessories.

steps.title: [DOI] Choose Location
steps.description: [DOI] Pick a container (pot, wooden box) or a corner of the yard for your fairy garden.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Soil and Base
steps.description: [DOI] Fill with potting soil. Add pebbles for a path. Create landscape with hills or flat areas.

steps.title: [DOI] Plant Greenery
steps.description: [DOI] Add small plants, moss, or ground cover. Think about scale - everything should be tiny!

steps.title: [DOI] Create Features
steps.description: [DOI] Make tiny houses from sticks, furniture from acorn caps, fences from twigs.

steps.title: [DOI] Add Inhabitants
steps.description: [DOI] Place fairy figurines, or leave empty for imagination! Add final decorative touches.

safetyNotes: [DOI] Small pieces can be choking hazards for young children

name: [DOI] Backyard Obstacle Course
description: [DOI] Design and conquer your own obstacle course! Jump, crawl, balance, and race through challenges.

steps.title: [DOI] Plan Your Course
steps.description: [DOI] Decide on 5-8 obstacles. Include different movements: jumping, crawling, throwing, balancing.

steps.title: [DOI] Set Up Obstacles
steps.description: [DOI] Examples: hoop jump, noodle limbo, cone weave, bucket ball toss, rope hop.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Start/Finish
steps.description: [DOI] Mark clear start and finish lines. Number obstacles if doing them in order.

steps.title: [DOI] Test Run
steps.description: [DOI] Run through once yourself. Is it challenging but doable? Adjust difficulty.

steps.title: Race!
steps.description: [DOI] Time yourself, race friends, or try to beat your own record!

name: [DOI] Leaf Rubbing Art
description: [DOI] Create beautiful impressions of leaves using crayons! A simple nature art activity perfect for all ages.

steps.title: [DOI] Collect Leaves
steps.description: [DOI] Gather leaves with interesting shapes and strong veins. Fresh leaves work best.

steps.title: [DOI] Position Leaves
steps.description: [DOI] Place leaves vein-side up under paper. Use tape to hold paper still.

steps.title: [DOI] Rub with Crayon
steps.description: [DOI] Rub crayon side across paper over leaf. The leaf pattern appears!
steps.tip: [DOI] Use side of unwrapped crayon for best results.

steps.title: [DOI] Create Composition
steps.description: [DOI] Make multiple rubbings to create a forest, tree, or abstract design.

steps.title: [DOI] Identify Leaves
steps.description: [DOI] Look up what trees your leaves came from. Label your artwork!

name: [DOI] Water Balloon Games
description: [DOI] Cool off with exciting water balloon games! From toss to relay races, get ready to get wet!

steps.title: [DOI] Fill Balloons
steps.description: [DOI] Fill balloons at outdoor faucet. Don't overfill - they pop too easily!

steps.title: [DOI] Balloon Toss
steps.description: [DOI] Partners toss balloon back and forth, stepping back after each catch. Who lasts longest?

steps.title: [DOI] Relay Race
steps.description: [DOI] Carry balloon on spoon across yard without dropping. Teams compete!

steps.title: [DOI] Target Practice
steps.description: [DOI] Draw chalk targets. Throw balloons at targets for points!

steps.title: [DOI] Clean Up
steps.description: [DOI] IMPORTANT: Pick up all balloon pieces - they're harmful to wildlife!

name: [DOI] Stargazing Night
description: [DOI] Explore the night sky! Learn constellations, spot planets, and maybe catch a shooting star.

steps.title: [DOI] Choose Location
steps.description: [DOI] Find dark spot away from lights. Backyard works if lights are off!

steps.title: [DOI] Let Eyes Adjust
steps.description: [DOI] Wait 10-15 minutes for eyes to adapt to darkness. Avoid looking at bright lights.

steps.title: [DOI] Find North Star
steps.description: [DOI] Locate Big Dipper, then follow pointer stars to Polaris, the North Star.

steps.title: [DOI] Hunt Constellations
steps.description: [DOI] Use star chart to find Orion, Cassiopeia, and seasonal constellations.

steps.title: [DOI] Watch for Movement
steps.description: [DOI] Satellites cross slowly. Shooting stars streak fast. Planets don't twinkle!

safetyNotes: [DOI] Stay in safe areas at night
